functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.fix.serialize_fix
documentation:
Sometimes python dictionary can not be serialized into
toml. Here fixing the commonly encountered issue:

- pathlib.Path (cast to string)
- None (cast to the string "__None__")

See: [deserialize_fix]()

functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.fix.serialize_fix_dict
documentation:
Applying [serialize_fix] to all values of the dictionary

See: [deserialize_fix_dict]()

functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.fix.deserialize_fix
documentation:
Casting the string "__None__" to None.

See: [serialize_fix]()

functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.fix.deserialize_fix_dict
documentation:
Applying [deserialize_fix]() to all values of the dictionary.

See: [serialize_fix_dict]().

functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.serialize._check_keys
documentation:
Raises an [improper_message.ImproperMessage]() error if any
key is missing from d.

functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.serialize.serialize
documentation:
Cast d to a string.
Counterpart: [deserialize]()

If 'token' is not None, its value will be added to the dictionary
under the key '__token__'. If this key already exists in d, a
ValueError will be raised.

functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.serialize.deserialize
documentation:
Cast message to a dictionary.
Counterpart: [serialize]().

Arguments
  message: the message to deserialize
  required_keys: if any of the keys is missing from the deserialized dictionary,
    an ImproperMessage error is raised
  token: if not None, the deserialized dictionary is expected to have a '__token__'
    key associated with this value. If not, an IncorrectToken error is raised.

functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.command.serialize_command
documentation:
Serialize a command to be executed on the system

Counterpart, see: [deserialize_command]().

Arguments
  command_id: unique identifier of the command
  command: the command to execute
  token: see [serialize.serialize]()

Returns
  The serialized message

functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.command.deserialize_command
documentation:
Deserialize a message sent by the server into a command to
be executed by the system.

Counterpart, see [serialize_command]()

Arguments
  message: the serialized message
  token: see [serialize.deserialize]()

Returns
  command_id: unique identifier of the command
  command: the command to execute


Raises
  [improper_message.ImproperMessage]() if message is not properly
  formated.

functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.command.serialize_command_result
documentation:
Serialize a command output to a string.

Counterpart method: [deserialize_command_result]().

Arguments
  result: output of the command
  token: see [serialize.serialize]()

Returns
  serialized result

functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.command.deserialize_command_result
documentation:
Deserialize a message into an instance of CommandResult

Counterpart method: [serialize_command_result]().

Arguments:
  message: the serialized message
  token: see [serialize.deserialize]()

Returns
  The corresponding instance of CommandResult

Raises
  [improper_message.ImproperMessage]() if message is not properly formatted.

classes.name: nightskycam_serialization.command.CommandResult
documentation: Summary of the "output" of a command executed on the system.

functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.config.serialize_config_update
documentation:
For server to request runners of remote system to reconfigure
themselves.

Counterpart: [deserialize_config_update]()

Arguments
  runner_name: name of the runner that needs reconfiguring
  config: the new configuration to apply.
  token: see [serialize.serialize]()

Returns
  corresponding serialized message

functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.config.deserialize_config_update
documentation:
For system receiving configuration update request for a runner.

Counterpart: [serialize_config_update]()

Arguments
  message: the serialized request
  token: see [serialize.deserialize]()

Returns
  Tuple:
  - the name of the runner that should update
  - the configuration

functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.status.serialize_status
documentation:
Serialize the dictionary with the keys "system" and "status".

Arguments
  system: the name of the nightskycam system
  status: the status dictionary to serialize
  token: the shared secret

Counterpart: [deserialize_status]().

See: [serialize.serialize]()

functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.status.deserialize_status
documentation:
Deserialize the message sent by a system, containing the status information
related to all runners executed on the system.

Returns:
  The name of the system
  The status dictionary for each runner (name of the runner as key)

Counterpart: [serialize_status]().

See: [serialize.deserialize]()

functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.status.has_runner_status_dict
documentation:
Returns True if the runner class has a corresponding implementation
of RunnerStatusDict, False otherwise.

functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.status.has_status_entries_report_function
documentation:
Returns True if the runner class has a corresponding implementation
of "get_report" function, False otherwise.

functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.status.get_runner_status_dict_class
documentation:
Returns the corresponding subclass of RunnerStatusDict, assuming
it is defined in this module and named '{runner class name}Entries'

functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.status.get_random_status_dict
documentation:
Arguments
  The name of a nightskycam runner class.

Returns
  An instance of the subclass of RunnerStatusDict corresponding
  to the class name passed as argument.

Raises
  TypeError if there is no instance of RunnerStatusDict corresponding
  to the class name passed as argument.

functions.name: nightskycam_serialization.status.get_status_entries_report
documentation:
Returns a summary reports for all status for which
a corresponding report function has been implemented using the
name 'get_{runner_class_name}_report'.

Arguments
  all_status: key: the runner name, value: the corresponding
    entries dictionary
